channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Smithsonian poised to shutter museums Sunday as shutdown showdown persists
item.description: (The Hill) -- The Smithsonian Institute announced Saturday that its museums will be closed starting Sunday as the lapse in government funding persists. "Due to the government shutdown, Smithsonian museums are temporarily closed beginning on Sunday, Oct.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: There are no survivors in the blast at a Tennessee explosives factory, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — The blast in rural Tennessee that leveled an explosives plant and was felt for miles around left no survivors, authorities said Saturday. The total number of dead was unclear, as was the cause of the Friday blast.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump directs Hegseth to use 'all available funds' to pay military workers amid shutdown
item.description: President Trump on Saturday directed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to use move all of the department's "available funds" to go toward paying military servicemembers during the government shutdown. Throwing blame toward congressional Democrats and naming Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — As National Guard troops arrive in Memphis, the memory of thousands of them with bayonetted rifles and tanks in 1968 is still fresh for Joe Calhoun. Back then, he marched in the streets with sanitation workers and the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Truce fizzles as U.S.-China trade tensions return to full boil
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For months, bubbling trade tensions between China and the U.S. seemed to calm to a simmer, with words like “thaw” and “truce” swapped in for warnings of economic “war.” Now, hostilities appear to be returning to full boil.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctor's double life exposed after hidden camera discovery leads to prison murder plot
item.description: Dr. Justin Rutherford is in prison after pleading guilty to sexually abusing his stepson and another minor. While awaiting trial, he attempted to arrange his stepson's murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy to treat his prostate cancer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Joe Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy as part of a new phase of treating the aggressive form of prostate cancer he was diagnosed with after leaving office, a spokesperson said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about the fraud case against New York attorney general Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James, a longtime political foe of President Donald Trump, was indicted by a federal grand jury Thursday on mortgage fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: BY PHILIP MARCELO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man convicted of killing a 15-year-old girl in her home in 2001 is executed by injection in Indiana
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was executed by injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year. Roy Lee Ward, 53, was put to death at the Indiana State Prison in Michigan City.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing 23-year-old Philadelphia woman harassed before disappearing: Police
item.description: She told family and friends before she disappeared that she was being harassed by unidentified people over the phone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: CDC panel to review childhood vaccine schedules
item.description: The latest workgroup will consider "new vaccine technologies and ingredients, and the rise of vaccine hesitancy.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is rolling out a new video of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown,
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma's GOP governor objects to Texas troops in Illinois
item.description: “Oklahomans would lose their mind if Pritzker in Illinois sent troops down to Oklahoma during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: Officials say an explosion at a Tennessee explosives plant has left 18 people missing and feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Political Power Ranker: Analyzing this year's biggest elections
item.description: On this week's edition of Political Power Ranker, we're breaking down some of the biggest races across the country.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: ICE agents shoot Chicago pastor in head with pepper ball
item.description: Pastor David Black of the First Presbyterian Church of Chicago is suing the Trump administration after being struck in the head with a pepper ball by ICE agents during a protest outside an ICE detention center in Broadview.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Jersey businessman who testified against ex-Sen. Bob Menendez won't go to prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New Jersey businessman who testified against former Democratic Sen. Bob Menendez and his wife at separate bribery trials won’t go to prison, after a judge credited him at his sentencing Thursday for showing honesty on the witness stand and sincere remorse.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top DHS official defends ICE agents filmed shooting pepper ball at pastor's head
item.description: (The Hill) – Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin on Wednesday defended an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer who shot a pastor with a pepper ball from a rooftop after video of the incident went viral this week. Videos of the Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Israel, Hamas agree to 'first phase' of plan to end fighting, release hostages, Trump says
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to the “first phase” of his peace plan to pause fighting and release at least some hostages and prisoners, U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New California law aims to improve school nutrition by phasing out some ultraprocessed foods
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California will phase out certain ultraprocessed foods from school meals over the next decade under a first-in-the-nation law signed Wednesday by Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Last of 10 New Orleans jail escapees from May is captured under a house in Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A monthslong search for the only Louisiana inmate still on the run after an audacious May jailbreak ended Wednesday when authorities say they found him hiding in a crawl space beneath an Atlanta home, bringing the last of the 10 escapees into custody.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Pentagon, journalists on collision course as restrictions on reporting loom
item.description: Facing a deadline next week on whether to sign a statement acknowledging new restrictions on how they do their jobs or risk being thrown out of the Pentagon, journalists who cover the U.S. military appear headed toward a showdown with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland guest dies after riding Haunted Mansion
item.description: A woman in her 60s suffered an apparent heart attack and died after riding the Haunted Mansion attraction at Disneyland on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry goes to discovery that could trap C02 and bring water to deserts
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Three scientists won the Nobel Prize in chemistry Wednesday for their development of new molecular structures that can trap vast quantities of gas inside, laying the groundwork to potentially suck greenhouse gases out of the atmosphere or harvest moisture from desert environments.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, STEFANIE DAZIO and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The shutdown is already causing flight delays. Service to more than 170 airports could stop entirely
item.description: Several major airports were experiencing delays on Wednesday, and smaller airports were at risk of losing service altogether by the end of the week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Authorities charge suspect, 29, with starting deadly Palisades Fire
item.description: Authorities arrested 29-year-old Jonathan Rinderknecht for lighting a fire on New Year’s Day that burned down much of the Pacific Palisades a week later.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, ABC 4

item.title: Joan Kennedy, first wife of Sen. Edward Kennedy, has died
item.description: Joan B. Kennedy, the former wife of Sen. Edward M. Kennedy who endured a troubled marriage marked by family tragedies, her husband’s infidelities and her own decades-long struggles with alcoholism and mental health, died on Wednesday. She was 89.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: GOP senators increasingly anxious about Trump’s aggressive use of National Guard
item.description: Republican senators are increasingly uneasy about President Trump’s standoff with Democratic governors over deploying National Guard troops from other states to Portland, Ore., and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: A New Orleans candidate's murder conviction was tossed but the state still challenges his past
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A down-ballot race for New Orleans clerk of criminal court has turned contentious, as candidate Calvin Duncan, who spent three decades in prison before his conviction was vacated, faces attacks from Louisiana’s attorney general and the incumbent clerk over whether he was truly exo
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Ross paintings to be auctioned to support public TV stations after federal funding cuts
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thirty paintings created by the bushy-haired, soft-spoken Bob Ross will soon be up for auction to defray the costs of programming for public television stations suffering from cuts in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California fire captain enlists motorists to lift downed chopper after hearing trapped medic groan
item.description: Just minutes after a medical helicopter crashed onto a California highway, Sacramento Fire Capt. Peter Vandersluis found himself directing an impromptu response team of about 15 drivers to lift the wreckage off a paramedic trapped underneath.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gold futures rise above $4,000 per ounce for the first time
item.description: Gold futures soared above $4,000 per troy ounce for the first time Tuesday, as many investors seek a safe place to park their money during the continuing U.S. government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Treasury Department defends Trump $1 coin plan
item.description: The Treasury Department defended its plans to potentially create a $1 coin featuring President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
